summary: Split the tail of lbmthp into a new matching lb_01F8 translation unit, corrected several lb data/symbol boundaries, and made small source/header fixes that improved matching for miscellaneous lb library data. The decomp.dev report shows +1232 matched code bytes, +480 matched data bytes, 13 new matches, and new 100% matches for lb_01F8 text, bss, and sdata2.
what_changed: Added a new file src/melee/lb/lb_01F8.c containing lbMthp8001F890, lbMthp8001F928, and lbMthp8001FAA0, which were removed from the end of src/melee/lb/lbmthp.c.

Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt so lbmthp.c ends at text 0x8001F890, bss 0x804335B8, and sdata2 0x804D7CE0, then added lb_01F8.c covering text 0x8001F890-0x8001FC08, bss 0x804335B8-0x80433658, and sdata2 0x804D7CE0-0x804D7CE8.
Added Object(Matching, "melee/lb/lb_01F8.c") to configure.py while lbmthp.c remains NonMatching.
Moved struct lbl_804333E0_t from lbmthp.static.h into lbmthp.h and kept a STATIC_ASSERT size check at 0x1D8 so the new lb_01F8.c can reference Movieplayer's type.
Kept lbl_804335B8_t local to the new lb_01F8.c with a STATIC_ASSERT size check at 0xA0 and an extern declaration for Movieplayer.
Changed THPVideoDecode's prototype from taking an s32 decode-buffer parameter to a void* parameter, and adjusted calls in lbmthp.c and lb_01F8.c accordingly.

Adjusted lbmthp matching details: used a pointer to Movieplayer rather than copying the struct in fn_8001E910, removed an extra newline from an HSD_ASSERTREPORT format string in fn_8001F13C, and used 0.0F literals instead of the old lbl_804D7CE0 float references in moved lb_01F8 functions.

Refined config/GALE01/symbols.txt data objects: split aggregate symbols such as lbSnap_803BAD00, str_arr_803BADB0, and str_arr_803BAE3C into individual lbl_* string symbols with explicit sizes and data:string or data:sjis annotations; also corrected lbShadow_803BA0FC size and added lbl_803BA12C.
Changed lb_00F9.c to define __FILE__ as "lbspdisplay.c", made two Vec3 constants static const, and swapped local far/ortho_bot initializers in lb_800138EC.

Adjusted small data-matching details in lbrefract.c and lbshadow.c by changing HSD_ASSERTREPORT condition arguments from NULL to 0 and, in lbshadow.c, changing the assertion message string to match the original data.
Updated lbsnap.static.h's Unk803BACC8 layout and initializer, replacing x1C with a void* plus x20 and pad fields to fit the corrected data layout.
smart_moves: Used a split-file approach for lbmthp: carving out the already-matchable 0x8001F890-0x8001FC08 range let lb_01F8 become a matching object without forcing the rest of lbmthp.c to match.

Aligned source splits, configure.py object status, and symbols.txt together; the PR did not only move code but also taught the build system and symbol map about the new translation unit and data boundaries.

Promoted the shared Movieplayer struct definition from a static header to the public lbmthp.h only because the new file needed the type, while leaving the Movieplayer storage itself static in lbmthp.static.h and referenced externally from lb_01F8.c.
Preserved struct layout confidence with STATIC_ASSERT checks for both lbl_804333E0_t and lbl_804335B8_t.

Converted broad data blobs and string arrays into precise symbol entries, including SJIS marking where applicable, which produced several small data-section matches across lbmthp, lbsnap, lbshadow, and lbrefract.
Fixed function prototypes to reflect actual pointer semantics, especially THPVideoDecode's decode buffer parameter, rather than relying on integer casts everywhere.
decomp_lessons: When a nonmatching file contains a contiguous range that can match independently, consider creating a new split/object for that address range instead of keeping the entire original file as one nonmatching unit.
A split change must be synchronized across splits.txt, configure.py, source files, and the symbol map; otherwise code/data ownership can become inconsistent.

Do not leave data as coarse str_arr_* or module-sized blobs once individual string boundaries are known. Splitting them into lbl_ADDRESS entries can unlock matched data and improve nearby code matches.
For cross-file statics, move only the type definition to a shared header when needed; keep actual storage and linkage decisions explicit.
Exact assertion strings and __FILE__ values affect rodata/data matching. Even punctuation, newlines, typos, and file-name macros may need to mirror the original binary.
Pointer-vs-integer prototype accuracy matters for matching. A wrong s32 parameter for a pointer can force noisy casts and can affect generated code.
Const qualification can move small vector data to the expected section and fix rodata matching.
naming_conventions: New split file used the address-style name lb_01F8.c corresponding to the 0x8001F890 range and existing lb naming practice.
Unidentified data/string symbols were named lbl_803Bxxxx rather than pretending semantic names, while known module symbols kept prefixes such as lbSnap_ and lbShadow_.
The public Movieplayer type retained the address-derived name struct lbl_804333E0_t because semantic field names remain mostly unknown.
The new local THP display/decode state type used struct lbl_804335B8_t and fields like x6C, x6E, x90, unk94, and unk98, preserving offset-based placeholder naming.
Function names for unknown lbmthp routines retained address suffixes, such as lbMthp8001F890, lbMthp8001F928, and lbMthp8001FAA0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Carved out lb_01F8 as a separate Matching object to isolate matchable functions from nonmatching lbmthp.c.
Used local variable/type tweaks for codegen: pointer-to-Movieplayer instead of struct copy, void* casts for THPVideoDecode, direct 0.0F literals, and precise u16 width/height handling.

Matched rodata/data by exact string content, including removing a newline from "filnum = %d, ofs = %d, by sugano." and reproducing lbshadow's original typo-like assertion string "coudn t get light position ...\n".
Set __FILE__ to "lbspdisplay.c" inside lb_00F9.c to reproduce original assertion/file string data despite the decomp file's current name.
Used const on static Vec3 literals in lb_00F9.c to place them in the expected read-only data section.
Modeled YUV texture state in lbl_804335B8_t with three GXTexObj fields and image pointers, matching the bss range 0x804335B8-0x80433658.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 66.55% (+0.03%, +1232 bytes), linked code 38.85% (+0.02%, +888 bytes), matched data 38.85% (+0.04%, +480 bytes), and linked data 33.15% (+0.01%, +168 bytes).

The bot listed 13 new matches, including lb_01F8 lbMthp8001F928, lbMthp8001FAA0, lbMthp8001F890, lb_01F8 .bss, lb_01F8 .sdata2, lb_00F9 .rodata, lbshadow .data/.sdata, lbsnap .data, lbrefract .sdata, and lbmthp fn_8001F13C/.sdata2.
follow_up_queries: Find other nonmatching files where a matching tail range can be split into an address-named Matching object.
Audit remaining str_arr_* symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt for possible splitting into exact string labels.
Search for THPVideoDecode declarations or calls that still treat pointer arguments as s32.
Check other lb assertion strings for exact punctuation, newlines, __FILE__ macro values, and typo preservation needed for data matching.
Review lbmthp remaining nonmatching functions after lb_01F8 was split out, especially fn_8001E910 and lbMthp_8001F410.
